examples: Numerical increases are used as quantifiable indicators of heightened interest in the new stimulus.
This practice, however, can lead to suboptimal results, as decision-makers tend to focus only on easily quantifiable factors.
I confess I enter this in a skeptical frame of mind, believing that we tend to get carried away in our desire to reduce everything to the quantifiable.
If this is, in fact, true, that mothers add a quantifiable value, why not tax it?
Uncertainty comes with a very real and quantifiable price tag an uncertainty tax, so to speak.
